dataset.description: Kidney segmentation from 3D transabdominal ultrasound images
dataset.acquisition_info: 3D transabdominal ultrasound using Siemens Acuson S3000 7CF1 HD probe (mechanically driven), isotropic voxel spacing of 0.3 mm, resolution 760 × 540 × (739 ± 55.15) voxels
dataset.annotation_info: Manual segmentation by two independent radiographers (10 and 11 years experience) using 3D Slicer, with gold-standard segmentations generated using STAPLE algorithm and validated by a senior radiologist
dataset.data_source: Single-center study (Nouvel Hôpital Civil of Strasbourg, France) - TRUSTED dataset
dataset.detailed_description: The TRUSTED dataset subset used in this study comprises 59 3D transabdominal ultrasound images of kidneys from 48 patients. This represents a subset of the full TRUSTED dataset, which originally included paired CT and US images. The ultrasound images were acquired using a Siemens Acuson S3000 7CF1 HD 3D probe with isotropic voxel spacing of 0.3 mm. Only images where the kidney was fully contained within the field-of-view and could be reliably segmented were included, excluding cases with severe artifacts. The segmentation task focuses on delineating the kidney parenchyma.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate kidney segmentation in ultrasound is important for organ volume measurement, disease monitoring (such as chronic kidney disease), and for inter-modal image registration applications in image-guided surgery and robotic navigation systems.
